question: why is ETT so important?
content:
E.T.T isn’t just a technique, it’s the silent engine behind every good shot. In bowling, people often obsess about the release, the revs… but none of that matters if the body and ball aren’t pointing in the right place from the very beginning.
Bowling punishes misalignment more than almost any other sport, that’s why E.T.T is so important.
Your free swing will always follow the direction of the pushaway.
You can be the strongest, most athletic bowler in the world, but physics wins every time.
If the pushaway is pointed straight → the swing becomes straight.
If it points sideways → the swing becomes sideways.
If the body is too twisted → the ball tracks that twist.
A swing is a pendulum, not a steering wheel. You don’t get to “force it back on track” and expect to repeat like a pro.
E.T.T is how you prevent the swing from going wrong in the first place.


E.T.T removes some typical amateur mistakes.
Some of the problems newer or inconsistent bowlers have come from misalignment:
swing behind the back
rounded/golf swing
topping the ball
bad accuracy
muscling the ball
E.T.T turns aim into something effortless
When your feet, hips, shoulders, hands, ball, pushaway, and swing all point toward the same line:
you don’t “try to hit the target”
the ball naturally wants to go there
accuracy becomes the result, not the effort
This is the closest thing bowling has to a “cheat code.”
It helps free the mind under pressure
When the mind is under stress, it wants to control things.
Trying to “guide” or “steer” the ball ruins shots and kills the swing.
But if your whole body starts correctly aligned:
you have less need to steer
you don’t need to add muscle
you trust the swing
you stay relaxed
E.T.T gives you a simple, reliable anchor when pressure tries to break you.
